model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: I’m taking Kentucky because Iowa State’s backcourt takes a major hit with lead guard Keshon Gilbert ruled out for the NCAA tournament (groin), which meaningfully lowers their creation/ball-security baseline in a Round of 32 game. Kentucky’s underlying profile is solid (KenPom #28; Torvik #32) with a balanced offense/defense that can win without needing extreme shooting variance. Iowa State still has a credible upset path if its defense controls tempo and it wins the turnover/3-point margins, but without Gilbert the Cyclones’ margin for error shrinks enough that Kentucky is the slightly safer advance.

reasoningStep.title: Round of 32: Kentucky vs Iowa State — availability swing favors Kentucky
reasoningStep.summary: With Iowa State missing its lead guard, Kentucky’s steadier two-way baseline becomes the safer pick in what otherwise profiles as a competitive matchup.
evidence: ESPN reported Iowa State lead guard Keshon Gilbert will miss the NCAA tournament with a groin injury (story id/44281982).
Kentucky baseline ratings: KenPom #28 (AdjEM 21.47; AdjO rank 39; AdjD 99.1) and Torvik #32 (OE rank 40; DE rank 32).
Iowa State’s upset path is still credible via defense/tempo control, but losing a primary initiator increases turnover risk and lowers late-clock shot creation.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: Kentucky injury report heading into NCAA Tournament: Lamont Butler, Trent Noah, Andrew Carr Skip to main content About Kentucky Wildcats News UK Basketball UK Football FanSided NCAA Sites Podcast Follow Us Share on X About Kentucky Wildcats News UK Basketball UK Basketball UK Basketball Recruiting UK Football FanSided NCAA Sites Podcast Wildcats Today Share on X Kentucky injury report heading into NCAA Tournament: Lamont Butler, Trent Noah, Andrew Carr The Cats have a long injury report but there is good news on the horizon. By Drew Holbrook | Share on Facebook Share on X Share on Reddit Copy Article Add us as a preferred source Mar 13, 2025; Nashville, TN, USA; Kentucky Wildcats forward Trent Noah (9) drives to the basket against the Oklahoma Sooners during the first half at Bridgestone Arena. Mandatory Credit: Steve Roberts-Imagn Images | Steve Roberts-Imagn Images As the 2025 NCAA Tournament tips off, the Kentucky Wildcats are limping—figuratively and, for some, literally—into March Madness. With a staggering 44 games missed due to injury across the roster this season, the Cats have faced a gauntlet of health setbacks. Yet, with the first-round matchup against Troy on the horizon, there’s cautious optimism in Lexington. Can this battered squad notch their first tournament win in two years and claw their way back to the Sweet 16 for the first time this decade? Let’s break down the health report and what it means for Kentucky’s postseason hopes. --> As the 2025 NCAA Tournament tips off, the Kentucky Wildcats are limping—figuratively and, for some, literally—into March Madness. With a staggering 44 games missed due to injury across the roster this season, the Cats have faced a gauntlet of health setbacks. Yet, with the first-round matchup against Troy on the horizon, there’s cautious optimism in Lexington. Can this battered squad notch their first tournament win in two years and claw their way back to the Sweet 16 for the first time this decade? Let’s break down the health report and what it means for Kentucky’s postseason hopes. The Injury Toll: 44 Games and Counting --> The Injury Toll: 44 Games and Counting Kentucky’s season has been a war of attrition. From sprained ankles to fractured feet, the Wildcats’ training room has seen more traffic than Rupp Arena’s concession stands. Those 44 missed games represent a roster stretched thin, forcing freshmen into big roles and veterans to grit through pain. It’s a testament to coach Mark Pope’s adaptability that Kentucky even made it to the dance—but now, the real test begins. --> Kentucky’s season has been a war of attrition. result.title: Iowa State Star Keshon Gilbert Ruled Out for NCAA Tournament
result.snippet: But when the Cyclones take the floor, both against Lipscomb and moving forward the rest of the tournament , they'll be without star guard Keshon Gilbert , who has been ruled out with the groin strain.

result.title: Iowa State's top playmaker Keshon Gilbert to miss NCAA tournament
result.snippet: Gilbert , who has been dealing with a groin injury, was out for four of the past seven games for Iowa State (24-9). result.title: Keshon Gilbert - Wikipedia
result.snippet: Keshon Montae Gilbert (born June 30, 2003) is an American basketball player for the Atlanta Hawks of the National Basketball Association, on a two-way contract with the College Park Skyhawks of the NBA G League. He played college basketball for the UNLV Runnin' Rebels and the Iowa State Cyclones.
